meta.title: path

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 2 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: path
root.mentor_note: path carries feeling into a word, whether shared, absent, or endured.

attempt.direction_prompt: Indifferent to the stranger’s pain, or moved by it?

context_sentences: The whole office signed a card expressing their sympathy after the sudden loss in his family.
She listened with real sympathy, though nothing she said could actually fix what had happened.
Good doctors, her mentor always said, need empathy at least as much as they need diagnostic skill.
The training focused less on procedure and more on empathy, on actually hearing what a patient was afraid of.
Voter apathy, not any single scandal, was blamed for the record low turnout.
His apathy toward the project was obvious from the first meeting, and it never really lifted.

construct_options.text: A lack of interest, enthusiasm, or concern about something
A strong, unshakeable feeling of anger
A deep, shared feeling of compassion for others

context_sentences: The lab’s pathology report finally explained symptoms three earlier tests had missed entirely.
Her interest in pathology began with a single undergraduate class on how tissue actually fails.

construct_options.text: The study of disease, or a harmful deviation from what is normal
A strong, unshakeable feeling of anger
A deep, shared feeling of compassion for others
